case_number: Criminal Bail Application No. S-1009 of 2024

chunks.text: IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR Cr. Bail App. No. S – 08 of 2026 (Imam Bux Rajper v. The State) Date of hearing: 12.02.2026 Date of decision: 12.02.2026 Mr. Shahid Hussain Gopang, Advocate along with applicant (present on interim pre-arrest bail). Syed Asghar Ali Shah Bukhari, Advocate along with complainant. Mr. Shafi Muhammad Mahar, Deputy Prosecutor General. O R D E R Mahmood A. Khan, J. – Present applicant seeks bail in Crime No.392 of 2024, registered at Police Station Shaheed Murtaza Meerani, District Khairpur, under Sections 364, 302, 120, 201, PPC, wherein though he is not nominated in the FIR, his name has appeared in further statement under Section 162, Cr.P.C. as such sent up for trial in the challan. 2. Learned Counsel for the applicant contends that a bald nomination against the present applicant is available in the further statement of the complainant, which was recorded one year after lodging of the FIR. He further states nine days’ delay without explanation is present in the FIR; that the co-accused Raja, who has been assigned the main role, has already been granted bail by way of order dated 29.01.2025 passed in Criminal Bail Application No. S-1009 of 2024 by this Court. He further states that against the present applicant, only the case of conspiracy being made available, he is entitled to bail. 3. Learned Counsel for the complainant, however, contends that the criminals in the matter have made sure that proper pursuance could not be made in the grant of bail of Raja, and that the medical is also available in the present matter, wherein conspiracy has taken place which was Cr. Bail App. No. S – 08 of 2026 Page 2 of 2 unearthed, and the present applicant, as such fully involved, is not liable to acquire the grant of bail. 4.

No. S – 08 of 2026 Page 2 of 2 unearthed, and the present applicant, as such fully involved, is not liable to acquire the grant of bail. 4. Learned DPG, however, contended that the FIR was lodged on 16.11.2024 and the statement, whereby the present applicant is said to have been involved, is coming up on 05.10.2025 on account of conspiracy alleged against him; the bail application is not opposed. 5. Having heard the learned Counsels and gone through the record, the role of conspiracy against the present applicant, which is said to have come out, entitles the present applicant for grant of bail, and accordingly, application stands allowed. The order dated 05.01.2026, whereby the applicant was admitted to interim pre-arrest bail, stands confirmed on the same terms and conditions. The bail application stands disposed of in the above terms. J U D G E Abdul Basit
